body:
HISTORICAL AUDIT 2026-07-21, angle H7. Both CONFIRMED, both recorded to the ledger and not queued until the PR-155 panel flagged the gap.

(1) PULL REPORTS A POST-DROP COUNT AS THE OFFER. pullFrom (src/federation/pull.ts:64-73) reconstructs each wire delta in a try/catch and drops failures on the floor. The array handed to federate is therefore ALREADY filtered, and federateImpl computes offered from what it was GIVEN, not from what the peer sent; rejected counts only verification and admission failures inside federate. A peer offering 100 deltas of which 40 will not reconstruct yields the report offered 60, accepted 60, rejected 0. There is no field, no log, and no return value anywhere carrying the 40.

The DROP is sanctioned by the deliberate-divergence note in offer.ts (a live peer stream may be partially good, and the next pull heals). Erasing the drop from the REPORT is not. The comment in pull.ts - federate counts what it admits - is precisely the mistaken inference: federate cannot count what never reached it. And because the failure is a property of the peer bytes rather than of timing, the next pull heals is FALSE here: every subsequent pull drops the same 40 and prints the same clean line.

FIX: count the drops in the catch and surface them. Either fold them into rejected and set offered to body.deltas.length so that offered equals accepted plus rejected becomes a checkable invariant, or widen FederationReport with an explicit unreconstructable field. cmdPull should name it.

(2) THE RUNNER PARTITION IS NOT A PARTITION. Runner.attach (src/runner/runner.ts:141-157) returns installed and skipped, documented as the bindings it could run and the ones whose implementation it lacks - a pair that reads as covering every binding the store holds. It does not: readBindingDefinitions drops candidates BEFORE attach sees them (a mistyped field hits a type-gate continue, an unparseable emit payload hits catch continue), so those names appear in neither list and installed plus skipped is less than the number of binding definitions in the ground. A deploy check of the form skipped is empty therefore passes while a typo-d definition sits inert, computing nothing. The comment at the drop site is correct about blast radius and silent about reporting.

FIX: have readBindingDefinitions report its drops - return the specs alongside a malformed list of deltaId and reason, or accept an onMalformed sink - and surface them as a third list on Runner, so the three together account for every binding delta in the ground.

RAILS - BOTH LEVELS for each. For (1): feed pullFrom an injected fetch returning a mix of good and corrupt wire deltas, assert the report accounts for EVERY delta the peer sent, and assert at the delta level which ones landed. For (2): plant a definition with an unparseable emit and assert attach NAMES it rather than omitting it.

CARE: both are REPAIRS of a reporting promise. Neither changes what the system serves.
title: Two completeness misreports with no ticket: federation pull hides unreconstructable deltas, and the runner drops malformed bindings from its accounting
